Lupus Patient Analysis — Century Health Take-Home

Data Analyst

2026-03-01

Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(broom)
library(testthat)
library(knitr)

source("R/helpers.R")
raw <- load_all_datasets("data")

# quick look at table sizes
for (nm in names(raw)) {
  cat(sprintf("%-12s %d x %d\n", paste0(nm, ":"), nrow(raw[[nm]]), ncol(raw[[nm]])))
}
## patients:    10000 x 27
## encounters:  104262 x 15
## symptoms:    1423 x 9
## medications: 22237 x 13
## conditions:  4149 x 6

1. Data Assessment

1.1 How the datasets connect

All five tables link through patient and encounter UUIDs:

                          ┌──────────────┐
                          │   PATIENTS   │
                          │ (PATIENT_ID) │
                          └──────┬───────┘
                                 │  1
              ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
              │                  │                  │
              ▼ *                ▼ *                ▼ *
     ┌────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
     │  ENCOUNTERS    │  │  SYMPTOMS    │  │  MEDICATIONS │
     │  (Id)          │  │  (PATIENT)   │  │  (PATIENT,   │
     │  FK: PATIENT   │  │              │  │   ENCOUNTER) │
     └───────┬────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────┬───────┘
             │ 1                                  │
             │            ┌──────────────┐        │
             └───────────►│  CONDITIONS  │◄───────┘
                     *    │  (PATIENT,   │    *
                          │   ENCOUNTER) │
                          └──────────────┘
From To Join Key
Patients → Encounters PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Symptoms PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Medications PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Patients → Conditions PATIENT_ID = PATIENT
Encounters → Medications Id = ENCOUNTER
Encounters → Conditions Id = ENCOUNTER

The raw encounters file is Parquet. Since arrow isn’t always available in R, I included a pre-converted encounters.csv in the submission. See R/helpers.R for how to regenerate it.

1.2 Data quality issues

# Issue Impact Fix
1 Mixed-case UUIDs across files Joins silently drop rows Lowercase everything
2 Same drug in 3+ casing variants Inflated med counts Uppercase + trim
3 GENDER is all NA in symptoms Can’t stratify by gender Backfill from patients
4 Dates stored as strings No time-based analysis Parse to Date/POSIXct
5 Symptom scores packed in one string Not usable for analysis Regex into 4 columns
6 Symptoms has duplicate rows per patient (Lupus + Anemia) Overstates N, mixes conditions Filter to Lupus, deduplicate
7 Mix of CSV, Parquet, XLSX Loading hassle Handled in load_all_datasets()

1.3 Two Lupus cohorts

There are two natural ways to define “Lupus patients” in this data, and they have different sizes:

Cohort Definition Used in
Diagnosed cohort Patients with at least 1 Lupus record in conditions Sections 3.1–3.3
Symptom-observed subset Diagnosed patients who also have a Lupus row in symptoms Sections 3.4, 4, 5

The symptom-observed subset is strictly contained within the diagnosed cohort. I’ll report the exact sizes after cleaning.

2. Cleaning

# 1) lowercase IDs
datasets <- normalise_ids(raw)

# 2) standardise med names
datasets$medications <- standardise_medication_names(datasets$medications)
cat(sprintf("Unique medication descriptions after cleaning: %d\n",
            n_distinct(datasets$medications$DESCRIPTION)))
## Unique medication descriptions after cleaning: 5
# 3) parse dates
datasets <- parse_dates(datasets)

# 4) parse symptom scores
datasets$symptoms <- parse_symptom_scores(datasets$symptoms)

# 5) keep only Lupus rows, one per patient
cat(sprintf("Symptoms before filter: %d rows, %d patients\n",
            nrow(datasets$symptoms), n_distinct(datasets$symptoms$PATIENT)))
## Symptoms before filter: 1423 rows, 948 patients
datasets$symptoms <- filter_lupus_symptoms(datasets$symptoms)
cat(sprintf("Symptoms after filter:  %d rows, %d patients\n",
            nrow(datasets$symptoms), n_distinct(datasets$symptoms$PATIENT)))
## Symptoms after filter:  948 rows, 948 patients
# 6) fill missing GENDER
datasets$symptoms <- fill_symptom_gender(datasets$symptoms, datasets$patients)
cat(sprintf("GENDER NAs remaining: %d\n", sum(is.na(datasets$symptoms$GENDER))))
## GENDER NAs remaining: 0
# quick preview
datasets$symptoms %>%
  select(PATIENT, PATHOLOGY, Rash, Joint_Pain, Fatigue, Fever) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  kable(caption = "Parsed symptom scores (first 5)")
Parsed symptom scores (first 5)
PATIENT PATHOLOGY Rash Joint_Pain Fatigue Fever
28d7b56c-6056-d0a2-2991-39d6e917216c Lupus erythematosus 34 39 9 12
6c434506-fb4b-3e3f-c19d-553dec3b6c17 Lupus erythematosus 19 44 48 15
44a8ca45-6c6e-38bb-fac0-ddbf7a7ee3a4 Lupus erythematosus 2 32 12 6
780ec78c-22a0-fcdb-17c6-ae9b2fcace9c Lupus erythematosus 30 30 41 19
cf5956bb-34f2-841b-2505-57b99991c377 Lupus erythematosus 28 26 33 5

Integrity tests

run_integrity_tests(datasets)

Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed Test passed

cat("\n**All tests passed.**\n")

All tests passed.

patients    <- datasets$patients
encounters  <- datasets$encounters
symptoms    <- datasets$symptoms
medications <- datasets$medications
conditions  <- datasets$conditions

# Diagnosed cohort: anyone with a Lupus condition record
lupus_patient_ids <- conditions %>%
  filter(str_to_upper(DESCRIPTION) == "LUPUS ERYTHEMATOSUS") %>%
  pull(PATIENT) %>%
  unique()

lupus_patients <- patients %>% filter(PATIENT_ID %in% lupus_patient_ids)

# Symptom-observed subset
symptom_patient_ids <- unique(symptoms$PATIENT)

n_total     <- n_distinct(patients$PATIENT_ID)
n_diagnosed <- n_distinct(lupus_patients$PATIENT_ID)
n_symptom   <- n_distinct(symptom_patient_ids)

tibble(
  Cohort     = c("All patients", "Diagnosed Lupus cohort", "Symptom-observed subset"),
  Definition = c("Everyone in patients.csv",
                 ">= 1 Lupus record in conditions",
                 "Diagnosed + has symptom data"),
  N          = c(comma(n_total), comma(n_diagnosed), comma(n_symptom))
) %>% kable(caption = "Cohort sizes")
Cohort sizes
Cohort Definition N
All patients Everyone in patients.csv 10,000
Diagnosed Lupus cohort >= 1 Lupus record in conditions 2,784
Symptom-observed subset Diagnosed + has symptom data 948

3. Descriptive Analysis

3.1 How many patients?

cat(sprintf("Total patients:                %s\n", comma(n_total)))
## Total patients:                10,000
cat(sprintf("Diagnosed with Lupus:          %s\n", comma(n_diagnosed)))
## Diagnosed with Lupus:          2,784
cat(sprintf("With symptom observations:     %s\n", comma(n_symptom)))
## With symptom observations:     948

There are 10,000 patients in the dataset overall. 2,784 have a Lupus diagnosis in the conditions table, and 948 of those also have symptom data. The rest of Section 3 uses the diagnosed cohort unless noted.

3.2 Medications over time

I’m counting only Lupus-directed prescriptions here — ones where REASONDESCRIPTION is “Lupus erythematosus.” This excludes things like Vitamin B12 and ferrous sulfate that are really for comorbid anemia.

lupus_meds <- medications %>%
  filter(PATIENT %in% lupus_patient_ids,
         str_to_upper(REASONDESCRIPTION) == "LUPUS ERYTHEMATOSUS")

meds_over_time <- lupus_meds %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(START, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(n_meds = n_distinct(DESCRIPTION), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(meds_over_time, aes(month, n_meds)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#2c7fb8", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(colour = "#2c7fb8", size = 1.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = pretty_breaks()) +
  labs(title = "Distinct Lupus medications per month (diagnosed cohort)",
       x = NULL, y = "Distinct medications") +
  theme_minimal()

3.3 Race and gender breakdown

race_data <- lupus_patients %>%
  count(RACE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n),
         label = sprintf("%s\n%.1f%%", RACE, pct * 100))

p1 <- ggplot(race_data, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = RACE)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, colour = "white") +
  coord_polar("y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(title = "By Race", fill = "Race") +
  theme_void()

gender_data <- lupus_patients %>%
  count(GENDER) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n),
         label = sprintf("%s\n%.1f%%", GENDER, pct * 100))

p2 <- ggplot(gender_data, aes(x = "", y = n, fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, colour = "white") +
  coord_polar("y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "By Gender", fill = "Gender") +
  theme_void()

p1 + p2

3.4 High-symptom patients

How many patients in the symptom-observed subset have all four symptom scores at 30 or above?

all_high <- symptoms %>%
  filter(Rash >= 30, Joint_Pain >= 30, Fatigue >= 30, Fever >= 30)

cat(sprintf("Patients with all 4 scores >= 30: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            nrow(all_high), nrow(symptoms),
            nrow(all_high) / nrow(symptoms) * 100))
## Patients with all 4 scores >= 30: 0 / 948 (0.0%)

4. Cohort Analysis

This section uses the symptom-observed subset (948 patients) since we need symptom scores for outcomes.

The plan is straightforward:

  • Classify each prescription into a therapy group
  • Figure out each patient’s primary therapy (most prescriptions; ties broken alphabetically)
  • Add age groups and composite symptom scores
  • Compare outcomes across therapy, ethnicity, and age group
lupus_meds <- lupus_meds %>% mutate(THERAPY = classify_therapy(DESCRIPTION))

cat("Therapy counts across all Lupus prescriptions:\n")
## Therapy counts across all Lupus prescriptions:
lupus_meds %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 10046
Cyclosporine (Immunosuppressant) 9094
Naproxen (NSAID) 2784 
# narrow to symptom-observed patients
sym_meds <- lupus_meds %>% filter(PATIENT %in% symptom_patient_ids)

# primary therapy: most prescribed, alphabetical tiebreak
patient_therapy <- sym_meds %>%
  count(PATIENT, THERAPY, name = "n_rx") %>%
  arrange(PATIENT, desc(n_rx), THERAPY) %>%
  group_by(PATIENT) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(PATIENT, THERAPY)

cat("Primary therapy distribution:\n")
## Primary therapy distribution:
patient_therapy %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666
Naproxen (NSAID) 282 
symptoms <- symptoms %>%
  add_composite_score() %>%
  mutate(AGE_GROUP = assign_age_group(AGE_BEGIN))

cohort <- symptoms %>% inner_join(patient_therapy, by = "PATIENT")

# only keep therapy groups with at least 10 patients
therapy_counts <- cohort %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE)
keep <- therapy_counts %>% filter(n >= 10) %>% pull(THERAPY)
cohort_main <- cohort %>% filter(THERAPY %in% keep)

cat(sprintf("Analysis cohort: %d patients\n", nrow(cohort_main)))
## Analysis cohort: 948 patients
cat("Included therapies:\n")
## Included therapies:
cohort_main %>% count(THERAPY, sort = TRUE) %>% kable()
THERAPY n
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666
Naproxen (NSAID) 282

4.1 Symptom severity by therapy and age

summary_age <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, AGE_GROUP) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(summary_age, aes(THERAPY, mean_score, fill = AGE_GROUP)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Mean composite score by therapy and age group",
       x = "Therapy", y = "Mean composite score", fill = "Age group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10, hjust = 1))

4.2 Symptom severity by therapy and ethnicity

summary_eth <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, ETHNICITY) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(summary_eth, aes(THERAPY, mean_score, fill = ETHNICITY)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Mean composite score by therapy and ethnicity",
       x = "Therapy", y = "Mean composite score", fill = "Ethnicity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10, hjust = 1))

4.3 Heatmap

cohort_main <- cohort_main %>%
  mutate(subgroup = paste(AGE_GROUP, "/", ETHNICITY))

heat <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY, subgroup) %>%
  summarise(mean_score = mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(heat, aes(subgroup, THERAPY, fill = mean_score)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", mean_score)), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ffffcc", high = "#d7191c") +
  labs(title = "Composite score heatmap: therapy x age/ethnicity",
       x = "Subgroup", y = "Therapy", fill = "Mean score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

4.4 Symptom distributions

long <- cohort_main %>%
  pivot_longer(c(Rash, Joint_Pain, Fatigue, Fever),
               names_to = "symptom", values_to = "score")

ggplot(long, aes(THERAPY, score, fill = THERAPY)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~symptom, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "Symptom distributions by therapy", x = NULL, y = "Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom")

4.5 What do we see?

overall_means <- cohort_main %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean = round(mean(COMPOSITE_SCORE), 1),
            sd = round(sd(COMPOSITE_SCORE), 1),
            .groups = "drop")

kable(overall_means, col.names = c("Therapy", "N", "Mean", "SD"),
      caption = "Composite score summary by therapy")
Composite score summary by therapy
Therapy N Mean SD
Naproxen (NSAID) 282 20.7 6.6
Prednisone (Corticosteroid) 666 20.9 6.3 
age_spread <- summary_age %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(range = round(max(mean_score) - min(mean_score), 1), .groups = "drop")

eth_spread <- summary_eth %>%
  group_by(THERAPY) %>%
  summarise(range = round(max(mean_score) - min(mean_score), 1), .groups = "drop")

cat("\nWithin-therapy spread across age groups:\n")
## 
## Within-therapy spread across age groups:
print(age_spread)
## # A tibble: 2 x 2
##   THERAPY                     range
##   <chr>                       <dbl>
## 1 Naproxen (NSAID)              2.5
## 2 Prednisone (Corticosteroid)   0.5
cat("\nWithin-therapy spread across ethnicities:\n")
## 
## Within-therapy spread across ethnicities:
print(eth_spread)
## # A tibble: 2 x 2
##   THERAPY                     range
##   <chr>                       <dbl>
## 1 Naproxen (NSAID)              0.3
## 2 Prednisone (Corticosteroid)   0.2

Looking at the results, there isn’t much differentiation between therapies — the means are close together and the standard deviations overlap. The within-therapy differences across age groups and ethnicities are also small (just a few points). The boxplots tell the same story: medians and IQRs overlap quite a bit. This is consistent with how Synthea generates data — symptom scores aren’t really driven by treatment assignment.

5. Statistical Modelling

The assignment asks for a model of whether symptoms decrease after medication. Unfortunately the symptom data is cross-sectional — each patient has one observation, not a before/after pair. There’s no way to measure change over time.

As a fallback, I’m modelling a cross-sectional outcome: whether Fatigue is below the cohort median (i.e. relatively low severity). This is weaker than what the assignment requests, but it’s the best I can do without fabricating a temporal structure that isn’t in the data.

Predictors: therapy type, age, gender, race, ethnicity, prescription count.

75/25 random split for train/test. Since we have one row per patient already, there’s no leakage concern.

rx_counts <- sym_meds %>% count(PATIENT, name = "n_rx")

model_df <- cohort_main %>%
  left_join(rx_counts, by = "PATIENT") %>%
  filter(!is.na(GENDER), !is.na(RACE), !is.na(ETHNICITY)) %>%
  mutate(
    LOW_FATIGUE = as.integer(Fatigue < median(Fatigue)),
    THERAPY   = droplevels(factor(THERAPY)),
    GENDER    = droplevels(factor(GENDER)),
    RACE      = fct_lump_min(factor(RACE), min = 20, other_level = "other"),
    RACE      = relevel(RACE, ref = "white"),
    ETHNICITY = droplevels(factor(ETHNICITY))
  )

cat("Race levels after collapsing sparse categories:\n")
## Race levels after collapsing sparse categories:
print(table(model_df$RACE))
## 
## white asian black other 
##   737    59   133    19
# drop any single-level factors (can't use in regression)
single_lvl <- names(model_df)[sapply(model_df, function(x) is.factor(x) && nlevels(x) < 2)]
if (length(single_lvl) > 0) {
  cat("Dropping single-level factors:", paste(single_lvl, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  model_df <- model_df %>% select(-all_of(single_lvl))
}
## Dropping single-level factors: GENDER
cat(sprintf("Modelling dataset: %d patients\n", nrow(model_df)))
## Modelling dataset: 948 patients
cat(sprintf("Fatigue median: %.0f\n", median(model_df$Fatigue)))
## Fatigue median: 26
cat("LOW_FATIGUE distribution:\n")
## LOW_FATIGUE distribution:
print(table(model_df$LOW_FATIGUE))
## 
##   0   1 
## 474 474
predictors <- c("AGE_BEGIN", "GENDER", "RACE", "ETHNICITY", "THERAPY", "n_rx")
predictors <- intersect(predictors, names(model_df))
fml <- as.formula(paste("LOW_FATIGUE ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
cat("Formula:", deparse(fml), "\n\n")
## Formula: LOW_FATIGUE ~ AGE_BEGIN + RACE + ETHNICITY + THERAPY + n_rx
fit <- glm(fml, data = model_df, family = binomial)
summary(fit)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = fml, family = binomial, data = model_df)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)                         0.522724   0.282669   1.849   0.0644 .
## AGE_BEGIN                          -0.005211   0.004871  -1.070   0.2847  
## RACEasian                           0.282064   0.275340   1.024   0.3056  
## RACEblack                          -0.445751   0.192060  -2.321   0.0203 *
## RACEother                          -0.351049   0.472861  -0.742   0.4578  
## ETHNICITYnonhispanic               -0.182730   0.132678  -1.377   0.1684  
## THERAPYPrednisone (Corticosteroid) -0.021092   0.213289  -0.099   0.9212  
## n_rx                               -0.021463   0.034908  -0.615   0.5387  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1314.2  on 947  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 1303.2  on 940  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 1319.2
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4

Odds ratios

or_tbl <- tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)

kable(or_tbl, digits = 3, caption = "Odds ratios (95% CI)",
      col.names = c("Term", "OR", "CI low", "CI high", "p"))
Odds ratios (95% CI)
Term OR CI low CI high p
(Intercept) 1.687 0.971 2.942 0.064
AGE_BEGIN 0.995 0.985 1.004 0.285
RACEasian 1.326 0.776 2.295 0.306
RACEblack 0.640 0.438 0.931 0.020
RACEother 0.704 0.269 1.767 0.458
ETHNICITYnonhispanic 0.833 0.642 1.080 0.168
THERAPYPrednisone (Corticosteroid) 0.979 0.644 1.488 0.921
n_rx 0.979 0.914 1.048 0.539

Train/test evaluation

set.seed(42)
idx <- sample(nrow(model_df), floor(0.75 * nrow(model_df)))
train <- model_df[idx, ]
test  <- model_df[-idx, ]

cat(sprintf("Train: %d, Test: %d\n", nrow(train), nrow(test)))
## Train: 711, Test: 237
fit_train <- glm(fml, data = train, family = binomial)

test$prob <- predict(fit_train, newdata = test, type = "response")
test$pred <- as.integer(test$prob >= 0.5)

cat("\nConfusion matrix:\n")
## 
## Confusion matrix:
print(table(Predicted = test$pred, Actual = test$LOW_FATIGUE))
##          Actual
## Predicted  0  1
##         0 38 27
##         1 89 83
acc <- mean(test$pred == test$LOW_FATIGUE)
cat(sprintf("\nAccuracy: %.1f%%\n", acc * 100))
## 
## Accuracy: 51.1%
# ROC curve
roc <- tibble(truth = test$LOW_FATIGUE, prob = test$prob) %>%
  arrange(desc(prob)) %>%
  mutate(tpr = cumsum(truth == 1) / sum(truth == 1),
         fpr = cumsum(truth == 0) / sum(truth == 0))

auc_val <- abs(sum(diff(roc$fpr) * (head(roc$tpr, -1) + tail(roc$tpr, -1)) / 2))

ggplot(roc, aes(fpr, tpr)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#2c7fb8", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = 0.3, label = sprintf("AUC = %.3f", auc_val), size = 4.5) +
  labs(title = "ROC curve — low fatigue classification",
       x = "False positive rate", y = "True positive rate") +
  theme_minimal()

Interpretation

Each coefficient represents the change in log-odds of low fatigue for a one-unit change in that predictor (holding others constant). The odds ratios are easier to interpret: OR > 1 means higher odds of low fatigue vs. the reference category, OR < 1 means lower odds. CIs crossing 1.0 mean not significant.

sig <- or_tbl %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)", p.value < 0.05)

cat("**Results:**\n\n")

Results:

if (nrow(sig) == 0) {
  cat("- None of the predictors are statistically significant at p < 0.05.\n")
  cat("- Therapy type, demographics, and prescription volume don't predict fatigue level in this cohort.\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("- %d predictor(s) significant at p < 0.05:\n", nrow(sig)))
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sig))) {
    cat(sprintf("  - **%s**: OR = %.2f (95%% CI: %.2f–%.2f, p = %.3f)\n",
                sig$term[i], sig$estimate[i],
                sig$conf.low[i], sig$conf.high[i], sig$p.value[i]))
  }
}
  • 1 predictor(s) significant at p < 0.05:
    • RACEblack: OR = 0.64 (95% CI: 0.44<U+2013>0.93, p = 0.020)
cat(sprintf("\n- **AUC = %.3f** — ", auc_val))
  • AUC = 0.515 <U+2014>
if (auc_val < 0.55) {
  cat("basically no better than flipping a coin.\n")
} else if (auc_val < 0.7) {
  cat("weak discriminatory power.\n")
} else {
  cat("moderate to good discriminatory power.\n")
}

basically no better than flipping a coin.

cat(sprintf("- **Accuracy = %.1f%%** on held-out test set.\n\n", acc * 100))
  • Accuracy = 51.1% on held-out test set.
cat("**Limitations:**\n\n")

Limitations:

cat("- This is cross-sectional, not longitudinal. I'm modelling whether fatigue is ",
    "below the median, not whether it improved after treatment. The assignment asks ",
    "for a pre/post comparison, but the data only has one observation per patient.\n")
  • This is cross-sectional, not longitudinal. I’m modelling whether fatigue is below the median, not whether it improved after treatment. The assignment asks for a pre/post comparison, but the data only has one observation per patient.
cat("- Patients weren't randomly assigned to therapies, so any associations could be confounded.\n")
  • Patients weren’t randomly assigned to therapies, so any associations could be confounded.
cat("- This is simulated Synthea data, so the lack of signal probably reflects the ",
    "data generation process more than anything clinical.\n")
  • This is simulated Synthea data, so the lack of signal probably reflects the data generation process more than anything clinical.
